summary: Promote the MPI-308 caption harness to a context-menu action (gallery card + history item, video variant = current frame) running as a NORMAL QUEUED generation with status-bar progress, caption injected into the prompt box positive field. Trigger moves off the prompt box chip; destination stays the prompt box. FORCES the text-only completion contract MPI-308 bypassed: generationService if(!urls.length) treats zero-media as CANCELLED and discards promptText, and this card cannot bypass it because it needs the queue+progress. Fix = op-level outputKind declaration, NOT a guard clause at the gate. Recon done: context menu items arrays are SEPARATE in MpiGalleryGrid.js ~1107 and MpiHistoryList.js ~229 (shared renderer, add entry TWICE); onSelect receives only key, parent block resolves filePath; _isVideo conditional pattern already exists; captureFrameBlob/captureSnapshot for video frames (must write to a real path, MpiLoadImageFromPath reads OS paths not blobs); injectPrompts for the write. NO work needed: StatusBar (short label only, caption goes to prompt box), progressStages (text workflow emits zero tqdm bars), queue panel (already falls back to a TEXT-labelled empty frame). OUT OF SCOPE: sidecar storage + auto-feeding upscale.

Replaced the caption->upscale gate with the real rationale. The captioner is NOT upscale-only: the primary workflow is describe -> EDIT the text -> generate (reference image -> caption -> adjust toward a target character -> paint face -> LoRA detail -> Head Swap app). The caption is an editable STARTING POINT, which is exactly why it belongs in the prompt box rather than stored and auto-consumed. This value is already demonstrated by the MPI-308 harness and does not depend on the unproven caption->upscale claim, so the card is no longer gated on that comparison. Upscale re-captioning stays a separate future consumer of the same op.

STEP 0 added and card flagged attention:required. The 5.24GB abliterated captioner weight fits NEITHER existing owner: not a MODEL (nobody generates with it; a ModelDef forces dead workflows/qualityTiers/mediaType fields) and not an APP (trigger is a context-menu action, not an App Library tile). USER DECIDED: introduce a third entity, a PLUGIN, with its own registry — { id, title, requiredDeps, operation }, nothing model-shaped. Rejected: ModelDef+isPlugin flag (dead fields, every model consumer must honour it), requiredDeps on the Head Swap app (wrong owner, general capability), reusing stock krea2-qwen3vl-clip (refusals return). MUST SOLVE: (1) install-on-demand gating since no model pulls it; (2) GC — both uninstall guards in routes/downloadManager.js build protected sets from MODELS unioned with _appRequiredDepIds(), so a dep owned by neither is INVISIBLE and any model uninstall deletes it; the plugin registry must be unioned into BOTH; (3) Pod — do not bake 5.24GB into the image for an optional captioner. Precedent: MPI-304 app requiredDeps, generalised one step. Dep entry still filed by KIND in assetDeps.js beside krea2-qwen3vl-clip.

note: todo -> doing. STEP 0 (plugin entity + GC guards + dep entry) built and tested.
All 6 steps shipped across 4 commits; awaiting one real in-app run.
